sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The click on the cross at (16, 14) caused a change. Looking at the image, I can see:
- The leftmost cross in the top grid now appears darker/different (it looks like a dark gray or charcoal color instead of light green)
- The cross to its right (at col 20) now has a gray cell in the middle where there was white before
This suggests that clicking on a cross "activates" it or changes its state. The pattern seems to be:
1. Click on a cross → it changes color (becomes darker)
2. The adjacent cell to the right also changes (becomes gray)
This is similar to level 1 where clicking on crosses caused them to merge. Let me continue clicking on the remaining crosses to see the pattern.
Looking at the image more carefully:
- The first cross (leftmost) is now dark gray/charcoal
- The cell to its right (which was white/gray) is now also gray
- This suggests the cross "expanded" or "merged" with the adjacent cell
Let me continue clicking on the other crosses to see if the same pattern holds.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking
seg = current_frame.segmentation
crosses = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'G', 'g', 'c'):  # light green, dark gray, gray, charcoal
        b = n['boundary']
        r = sum(p[0] for p in b)//len(b)
        c = sum(p[1] for p in b)//len(b)
        print(f"Object at ({r}, {c}), color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, id={n['id']}")

# Let's also check the top grid area specifically
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Top grid row 16 (cols 6-50) ===")
print(f"16: {ascii_lines[16][6:52]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
